result.stdout:
Usage: Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll [command] [options]

Options:

  -v | --version
  Show version information

Commands:

  delete
  list
  purge
  upsert
  use

Use "Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll [command] --help" for more 
information about a command.

result.stdout:
Usage: Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll delete [options]

Options:

  The name of the connection to delete

commands.payload:
Usage: Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll delete [options]

Options:

  --name  <TEXT>
  The name of the connection to delete

result.stdout:
Usage: Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll upsert [options]

Options:

  --skip-connection-test
  Test the Dataverse connection after upsertion

  --name                   <TEXT>
  The name to identify the connection

  --type                   <CONNECTIONTYPE>
  Specify the connection type to authenticate against the Dataverse environment
  Allowed values: UserPassword, ClientCertificate, ClientSecret, Unknown, 
Interactive, DeviceCode, ManagedIdentity, AzureDefault, AzureCli, 
AzureDeveloperCli, AzurePowershell, VisualStudio, VisualStudioCode

  --environment-url        <URI>
  The URL of the Dataverse environment

  --user-name              <TEXT>
  The username to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --password               <TEXT>
  The passwort to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --application-id         <TEXT>
  The application id to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --client-secret          <TEXT>
  The client secret to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --tenant-id              <TEXT>
  The tenant id to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --certificate-file-path  <TEXT>
  The absolute path to the certificate to authenticate against the Dataverse 
environment

  --certificate-password   <TEXT>
  The certificate password to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

commands.payload:
Usage: Empowered.Dataverse.Connection.Tool.dll upsert [options]

Options:

  --skip-connection-test
  Test the Dataverse connection after upsertion

  --name                   <TEXT>
  The name to identify the connection

  --type                   <CONNECTIONTYPE>
  Specify the connection type to authenticate against the Dataverse environment
  Allowed values: UserPassword, ClientCertificate, ClientSecret, Unknown, 
Interactive, DeviceCode, ManagedIdentity, AzureDefault, AzureCli, 
AzureDeveloperCli, AzurePowershell, VisualStudio, VisualStudioCode

  --environment-url        <URI>
  The URL of the Dataverse environment

  --user-name              <TEXT>
  The username to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --password               <TEXT>
  The passwort to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --application-id         <TEXT>
  The application id to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --client-secret          <TEXT>
  The client secret to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --tenant-id              <TEXT>
  The tenant id to authenticate against the Dataverse environment

  --certificate-file-path  <TEXT>
  The absolute path to the certificate to authenticate against the Dataverse 
environment

  --certificate-password   <TEXT>
  The certificate password to authenticate against the Dataverse environment
